name: Libgen: Book Download
summary: Search a Libgen mirror for a book, pick the best match in the requested format (default epub), resolve its direct download URL, and emit it as attachment_urls for --download-dir.
description: End-to-end: drives the Libgen homepage search box, reads the results table, selects the best candidate whose extension matches `format` (default epub, optionally constrained by `language`, choosing the `result_index`-th match), then resolves the file's real download link from the mirror's own ads.php page via a same-origin fetch and returns it as `attachment_urls` (plus `download_url` and chosen metadata). Pair with `--download-dir <folder>` so the CLI downloads the file into <folder>/attachments/. Returns found=false with a status/error when no matching format or download link is found, so callers can flag and retry with another base_url/format.

query.description: Book to find: title, author, or both (e.g. 'Brave New World Huxley'). Searches all columns.

base_url.description: Base URL of the Libgen mirror (libgen.li-family layout). Defaults to https://libgen.li/. Override when the domain rotates (https://libgen.gs/, https://libgen.la/, ...). Include the trailing slash.

format.description: Desired file extension, matched exactly against the Ext. column. Defaults to epub. Common: epub, pdf, mobi, azw3, djvu.

language.description: Optional language preference (e.g. English). When set and at least one matching-format row has that language, selection is restricted to it; otherwise ignored.

result_index.description: Which matching candidate to pick (0 = first/best). Use libgen/search first to inspect candidates, then pick a different index here.

steps.name: Navigate to the Libgen mirror homepage
Type the query into the search box
Pick the best format match and resolve its download URL

help.summary: Find a book on Libgen and resolve its direct download URL (default epub). Use with --download-dir to save the file.

parameters.result_index: Which matching candidate to pick (0 = first).

examples.command: rzn-browser run libgen download --param query="Brave New World Huxley" --download-dir ~/Books/bnw

examples.description: Prefer a PDF, English, on a different mirror
examples.command: rzn-browser run libgen download --param query="Sapiens Harari" --param format=pdf --param language=English --param base_url="https://libgen.gs/" --download-dir ~/Books/sapiens
rzn-browser run libgen download --param query="Dune Herbert" --param result_index=1 --download-dir ~/Books/dune

returns: Object: { found, status, chosen{title,author,...,md5}, download_url, attachment_urls:[{url,filename}] }. Run with --download-dir <folder> to save the file to <folder>/attachments/.

found=false carries status (no_table | no_format_match | no_download_link) and error; no_format_match also lists available_formats so you can retry with a different format. no_table usually means base_url is not a libgen.li-family mirror — try another base_url.
notes: Resolves the file's real link from the mirror's own ads.php page (same-origin fetch with session cookies) to get a fresh download key.
The actual file fetch is performed by the CLI when you pass --download-dir; it requires network access from the same machine/IP that resolved the link.
Search drives the homepage box (default form searches title/author/series/year/publisher/isbn).
Targets the libgen.li-family layout (libgen.li/.gs/.la/.vg/.pm). The libgen.rs/.is family uses a different page structure and is not supported by this workflow.
No CDP; all JS runs in the page main world.
